subject: History
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the transformation of Samantas into independent dynastic rulers
Examine the socio-political significance of rituals like Hiranya-garbha
Understand the administrative machinery and taxation systems of medieval kingdoms
Evaluate the 'Tripartite Struggle' as a conflict for resources and prestige
Describe the architectural and artistic achievements of the Chola dynasty
Analyze the sophisticated irrigation and agricultural developments in the Kaveri delta
Explain the democratic and committee-based functioning of the Chola Sabha

lesson_plan.title: Emergence of New Dynasties
topics_covered: The role of Samantas in the 7th century
Case Study: Rashtrakutas gaining independence from Chalukyas
Rituals of legitimacy: Hiranya-garbha (The Golden Womb)
teaching_strategy: Discussion: 'How does a subordinate become a master?'
Analysis of the use of Brahmanas to gain Kshatriya status
Mapping the Gurjara-Pratiharas, Palas, and Rashtrakutas
student_activities: Locate the three major new dynasties on a blank map of India
Write a short paragraph explaining the significance of the Hiranya-garbha ritual
assessment: Identification of the original professions of the Kadamba and Gurjara-Pratihara founders

lesson_plan.title: Administration in the Kingdoms
topics_covered: Royal Titles: Maharaja-adhiraja and Tribhuvana-chakravartin
Resources and Revenue: The 400 types of taxes (Vetti and Kadamai)
Utilization of wealth: Temples, Forts, and Warfare
teaching_strategy: Categorizing medieval taxes: Forced labor vs. Cash/Kind
Flowcharting the distribution of revenue from peasant to king
Discussion on why positions in the army were often hereditary
student_activities: Create a 'Tax Chart' showing the different groups who paid revenue to the king
Compare medieval tax collection with modern government taxation
assessment: Listing three purposes for which the king's resources were used

lesson_plan.title: Prashastis and the Rewards of Knowledge
topics_covered: Content and nature of Prashastis
Land grants (Brahmadeya) and copper plate inscriptions
Kalhana's 'Rajatarangini' as a critical historical source
teaching_strategy: Analyzing a sample Prashasti of Nagabhata
Contrast study: Court poets vs. Kalhana’s critical history of Kashmir
Visual analysis of the royal seals on copper plates
student_activities: Identify what kings gave to Brahmanas in land grants (based on the textbook description)
Discuss: 'Why did Kalhana differ from other court poets?'
assessment: Matching: Kings vs. their achievements mentioned in Prashastis
Short note on the information recorded on copper plates

lesson_plan.title: Warfare for Wealth and Territory
topics_covered: The Tripartite Struggle for Kanauj
Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni’s raids on religious centers
Al-Biruni’s 'Kitab-al-Hind' and Chahamana (Chauhan) expansion
teaching_strategy: Analyzing why Kanauj was the 'prize' of North India
Discussion on the targeting of temples during medieval warfare
Timeline of Muhammad Ghori vs. Prithviraj III
student_activities: Trace the path of Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni from Afghanistan to Somnath
Write a summary of Al-Biruni's contribution to Indian history
assessment: Comparing the outcomes of the First and Second Battles of Tarain

lesson_plan.title: The Splendour of the Cholas
topics_covered: The rise of the Cholas: Vijayalaya to Rajendra I
Temple Architecture: Thanjavur and Gangaikonda-cholapuram
Chola Bronze sculpture: Process and Significance
teaching_strategy: Visual presentation of Chola temples as social and economic hubs
Explaining the 'Lost Wax' technique (briefly) used for bronze statues
Discussion on how Rajendra I used the navy to expand to Southeast Asia
student_activities: Sketch a simple diagram of a Chola temple showing it as a nucleus of settlements
Find the Kaveri River and Thanjavur on a map of South India
assessment: Describing the role of Chola temples beyond worship
Identifying the unique feature of Chola Bronze images

lesson_plan.title: Agriculture and Irrigation in the Chola Kingdom
topics_covered: Clearing of forests and leveling of land in the Kaveri delta
Techniques: Embankments, Canals, and Wells
Role of the king and villagers in planning water resources
teaching_strategy: Diagrammatic explanation of the canal system in the delta
Discussion on the necessity of two crops a year in the South
Analyzing the 'state-led' efforts in large-scale irrigation
student_activities: List four different methods of irrigation mentioned in the Chola records
Write a short note on why the Kaveri delta was ideal for agriculture
assessment: Explaining the term 'Vadi-vaykkal' (if applicable) or general Chola irrigation
True/False: Did Chola kings take a personal interest in irrigation?

lesson_plan.title: Administration of the Chola Empire
topics_covered: Settlements of peasants (Ur) and groups of villages (Nadu)
The Sabha and its committees (Variyams)
The Uttaramerur Inscriptions: Qualification for membership
teaching_strategy: Role-play of the 'Pot-ticket' (Kudavolai) system of election
Analyzing the strict criteria for joining the Sabha
Review of the chapter through a mind map
student_activities: List the qualifications required to become a member of a committee in the Chola Sabha
Categorize the different committees: Gardens, Temples, Irrigation
assessment: Evaluation of the 'Qualifications list' based on the Uttaramerur inscription

teaching_aids: NCERT History Textbook: Our Pasts - II
Images of the Chola Nataraja and Thanjavur Temple
Photographs of copper plates and inscriptions
evaluation_methods: Comparative analysis of administration (Chola vs. North Indian)
teacher_tip: When teaching about the Cholas, emphasize that the temple was a 'bank' and an 'employer'. This helps students move beyond the idea of temples as just religious buildings and see them as economic powerhouses.
